class.name: FabricZone
class.brief_description: Headless zone server with entity stepping, ghost-based interest culling, and STAGING migration.
class.description: Headless [SceneTree] main loop that manages a fixed-size pool of entities partitioned across zones by 30-bit Hilbert code (Skilling 2004). Each zone iterates exactly [code]ZONE_CAPACITY[/code] slots per physics frame regardless of occupancy (constant-work design). Entities that cross zone boundaries migrate via the STAGING protocol: [code]OWNED(A) -> STAGING(A,B) -> OWNED(B)[/code]. The core broadphase and ghost-expansion algorithms are formally verified in Lean 4 and code-generated into [code]predictive_bvh.h[/code] (R128 64.64 fixed-point, micrometers). All spatial computations go through the R128 bridge at micrometer precision, while entity state uses [code]real_t[/code]. Every entity shares a 100-byte snapshot on CH_INTEREST and CH_PLAYER: [codeblock] Bytes 0--3 global ID (u32) Bytes 4--27 position cx, cy, cz (3 x f64) Bytes 28--39 velocity/acceleration (6 x int16, V_SCALE / A_SCALE) Bytes 40--43 hybrid logical clock (24-bit frame | 8-bit sub-counter) Bytes 44--99 payload (14 x u32) [/codeblock] Payload word 0 layout: class tag (bits 24--31), owner/player ID (bits 0--15), state flags (bits 16--23). Class tags: 0 NPC, 1 player, 2 prop, 3 effect, 4 humanoid. Player entities use [code]global_id = PLAYER_ENTITY_BASE + player_id[/code] (2,000,000+) and expire after three seconds without a CH_PLAYER update. Pen-stroke knots use [code]global_id >= STROKE_ENTITY_BASE[/code] (1,000,000+) and pack RGBA8888 color and anchor positions into payload words 4--13. Class-4 humanoid entities extend the same payload region with 55 muscle-triplet bone rotations (swing-twist Vector3 in [code][-1, 1][/code]). [b]Zone server mode[/b] (default): [codeblock] godot --headless --main-loop FabricZone -- --zone-id 0 --zone-count 3 --scenario concert [/codeblock] [b]Player client mode:[/b] Pass [code]--player-id N[/code] to connect to a target zone via ENet, publish player state on CH_PLAYER (channel 2), and drain interest snapshots from CH_INTEREST (channel 1). [codeblock] godot --headless --main-loop FabricZone -- --player-id 0 --base-port 17500 [/codeblock] [b]Graceful shutdown:[/b] The drain protocol (DRAIN_MAGIC on CH_MIGRATION) force-migrates all entities to zone 0, which writes a [FabricSnapshot] Resource to disk. On restart, [code]_load_snapshot[/code] redistributes entities across the new zone topology. [b]Crash recovery (journal):[/b] [code]FabricZoneJournal[/code] writes discrete entity mutations — spawn, despawn, payload_update — to a per-zone SQLite database ([code]fabric_journal_N.db[/code] in the user data directory). On restart, [code]replay()[/code] restores dynamic entities (humanoid players, asset instances) on top of the deterministic static-world initialization. Physics-step position updates are not journaled because they are deterministic from the simulation. Periodic full-snapshot records prune the mutation log and bound replay time. WAL mode ([code]PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL[/code]) is used when available for better crash-consistency.

method.name: hilbert_cell_of_aabb
method.description: Returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the Hilbert cell identified by [param code] at the given [param prefix_depth] (bits of precision, 1--10). The AABB is in simulation-space meters ([code][-15, 15][/code] per axis). Used by [code]zone_curtain.gd[/code] for label placement and boundary visualization.

method.name: hilbert_of_point
method.description: Returns the 30-bit Hilbert code for the simulation-space position ([param x], [param y], [param z]) in meters. Delegates to the same R128-precision path used internally by entity zone assignment, so the result is exact.
